cards.back: Data hiding in text-basic features refers to the use of algorithms and techniques to embed hidden information within plain text, making it difficult to detect without proper knowledge.
Data hiding has various applications, including copyright protection, digital watermarking, secure communication, and steganography.
Watermarking is a technique used to embed a hidden signature or pattern within a digital image or video, allowing for the identification of its origin or authenticity.
Intuitive methods for data hiding include using plain text to embed hidden information, where the hidden data is not immediately apparent to the casual observer.
Simple digital methods for data hiding include using Least Significant Bit (LSB) substitution, where the least significant bit of each pixel is replaced with the hidden data.
